_notes: Auto-generated by scripts/classify_districts.py (Stage 4) produced an EMPTY rule set, and human review 2026-07-19 confirms that is STRUCTURALLY CORRECT for most of this pack, not a labeling gap: Suffolk's Unified Development Ordinance concentrates every base district's regulation into single shared sections scraped as one source each -- SEC. 31-402 establishes all 32 districts (C/A/RE/RR/RL/RLM/RM/RC/RU/RU-12/RU-18/RU-24/MUC-40/B-1/B-2/O-I/CP/M-1/M-2/CBD/VC/PD/TND plus overlays F/CB/HC/SCOD/AO/MUD, FRRD, W), SEC. 31-403 holds every district's purpose statement, SEC. 31-406 is the all-district Use Matrix (Table 406-1, 22 district columns), SEC. 31-407 the all-district dimensional table, and SEC. 31-408 the per-district criteria for all 14+ base districts inside one source. Unlike lynchburg/salem/staunton (one chapter per district, keyable by numeric prefix) there is NO title-keyable section that regulates exactly one base district, so a source-level rule would mislabel multi-district text; those sections correctly stay unknown/general and district-aware retrieval for Suffolk rests on gis_verified parcel fixtures rather than chunk tags (same trade-off as the chesapeake/newport-news giant-use-table packs, where the offline keyword recall floor is set low and live vector recall is the quality signal). The ONLY two single-district sections in the pack get rules: SEC. 31-419 Mixed Use Development Overlay District (MUD) and SEC. 31-420 Fairgrounds Revitalization and Redevelopment District (FRRD) -> both mixed-use-core (FRRD's own purpose text: 'residential, commercial, mixed-used office/commercial/residential, and institutional use development' of the Fairgrounds area of Downtown Suffolk; MUD is literally the Mixed Use Development overlay -- the portsmouth NMU/GMU/MU-H named-mixed-use precedent). Both codes appear as real mapped polygons in the city's GIS (Zoning_Suffolk layer, Locality_Zones FeatureServer/11 CODE values MUD and FRRD, services.arcgis.com/ex1Az95h0eiponPK -- the same regional Hampton Roads org that hosts the hampton-va layer; roster matches SEC. 31-402 exactly). Article scoping: this pack's top-level manifest 'article' fields are null; article_contains matches breadcrumb[1] = 'ARTICLE 4 - ZONING' (read from source.metadata['breadcrumb'] at runtime per app/source_classifier.py, same as salem-va/staunton-va). Other candidates considered and rejected: 31-410 PD spans residential/commercial/industrial planned developments (no single vocab term), 31-411 Use Patterns covers cluster/hamlet/TND patterns in one source, and the remaining Article 4 overlay sections (SCOD/HC/AO/CB/F/W, 31-412 through 31-418) are environmental/procedural overlays spanning heterogeneous base districts (the salem/staunton overlay precedent: leave unmapped).
